name: Overall LEV Outlook
summary: Current public outlook for overall progress toward longevity escape velocity.
main_blockers: The field still lacks replicated human functional gains that clearly stack toward long-run LEV.
Many track baselines remain low-confidence, biomarker-heavy, animal-stage, or disease-specific rather than direct aging efficacy.
best_current_signals: All 38 seeded tracks now have public baseline outlooks, so the public map is broad enough for disciplined surveillance.
Multiple hallmarks now include human biomarker, functional, or disease-outcome signals, even though most remain low-confidence.
forecast_note: The June baseline pass makes the public map more complete, not more optimistic. The center of gravity remains early human translation: enough human biomarker, functional, and disease-specific outcome evidence to track seriously, but not enough replicated aging-directed functional benefit to imply escape velocity.
